plan_metadata.name: Feature Set Sub Id And Rg Name

steps.description: Click on the "Declarative Agent" option within the "New Project" dropdown menu to create an agent using instructions and actions.
Click on the "General Teams Agent" option within the dropdown menu titled "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" at coordinates (474, 210).
Click on the dropdown option "Tab" under the "Teams Capability" menu in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "Default folder" dropdown input field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select or specify the project directory.

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
env_file="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_wj4gt/env/.env.dev"

if [ ! -f "$env_file" ]; then
    echo "Error: File $env_file does not exist."
    exit 1
fi

sed -i '/^AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID=/d' "$env_file"
sed -i '/^AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME=/d' "$env_file"
echo "AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID=38e7c68a-ca46-4348-91ce-dadaf756999a" >> "$env_file"
echo "AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME=test" >> "$env_file"

cat "$env_file"
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0

=== STDOUT ===
# This file includes environment variables that will be committed to git by default.

# Built-in environment variables
TEAMSFX_ENV=dev
APP_NAME_SUFFIX=dev

# Updating AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID or AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME after provision may also require an update to RESOURCE_SUFFIX, because some services require a globally unique name across subscriptions/resource groups.
RESOURCE_SUFFIX=

# Generated during provision, you can also add your own variables.
TEAMS_APP_ID=
AZURE_APP_SERVICE_RESOURCE_ID=
TAB_ENDPOINT=
AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID=38e7c68a-ca46-4348-91ce-dadaf756999a
AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME=test

✓ Script executed successfully
=== STDERR ===

Success/failure status

steps.description: Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code, within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace interface.
Type text 'provision' into the Visual Studio Code command palette to search for the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Provision" command in the VS Code command palette after searching for "provision", as indicated by the red crosshair on the highlighted dropdown suggestion.
Click the "Provision" button in the confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code to authorize resource provisioning using the selected Azure and Microsoft 365 accounts.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code while the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface is active.
Type 'notifi' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input box to filter available commands.
Click the "Notifications: Show Notifications" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette, indicated by the crosshair at (301, 119).

parameters.sample:
=== Generated Script ===
Language: bash

```bash
env_file="/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_wj4gt/env/.env.dev"

# Ensure file exists
if [ ! -f "$env_file" ]; then
  echo "File not found: $env_file"
  exit 1
fi

# Set AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID
sed -i '/^AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID=/d' "$env_file"
echo "AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID=38e7c68a-ca46-4348-91ce-dadaf756999a" >> "$env_file"

# Set AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME (empty value)
sed -i '/^AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME=/d' "$env_file"
echo "AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP_NAME=" >> "$env_file"

# Show result
echo "=== File Content ==="
cat "$env_file"
```

=== Execution Results ===
Exit Code: 0
